source_summary.official_box_source: NBA official scorer report uploaded by user

game_phase_analysis.early_game: San Antonio opened with a Wembanyama pressure burst and Minnesota missed its first 12 shots before stabilizing through Edwards shot-making and offensive rebounding.
game_phase_analysis.second_quarter: Minnesota fought back to a 51-51 halftime tie behind Edwards, McDaniels, Gobert putbacks, and second-chance pressure.
game_phase_analysis.third_quarter: Critical model phase. SAS won the 3Q 35-28 behind 6-of-10 three-point shooting and cleaner halfcourt spacing. This created the live total danger zone and pushed SAS back outside the 5.5 spread pocket.
game_phase_analysis.fourth_quarter: Wembanyama controlled the close despite foul pressure, scoring 16 in the 4Q. Minnesota cut it to one at 99-98, but SAS answered through Champagnie spacing, Wembanyama isolation/three-point shot-making, and late free throws.

liveflow_investments.selection: No - San Antonio wins by over 5.5 points
liveflow_investments.diagnostic: Correct competitive-game thesis, but Wembanyama 4Q scoring and SAS late free throws broke the 5.5 margin band.
Good damage-control exit. 3Q pace spike and SAS efficiency flipped under probability negative before the final went over.
Hook-style live prop miss. Fox had 3 by end of 3Q but logged zero rebounds in Q4 as Wembanyama/Champagnie controlled late defensive boards.

liveflow_result_summary.notes: One cashout avoided full -5.00 total loss but overall slate remained negative due to spread margin and Fox rebound miss.

model_hits: Pregame competitive-game thesis was closer than Game 2 blowout profile; Wolves kept the game within single digits and had a late one-possession window.
Cashing out total under was correct once 3Q shot quality and pace moved against the position.
Wembanyama offensive takeover and foul-risk resilience were correctly identified as central forward variables.
model_misses: Spread ticket underestimated Wembanyama fourth-quarter closeout scoring and Spurs late free-throw margin expansion.
Fox rebound 4+ overestimated guard rebound accumulation after halftime; late boards consolidated to Wembanyama and Champagnie.
Total under entry did not carry enough buffer against 3Q shooting acceleration and playoff close-game free throws.

patch_queue_additions.trigger: If SAS leads by 1-7 entering final 6:00 and Wembanyama is active despite foul trouble, widen SAS margin distribution by +1.5 to +2.5 points.
For guard rebound ladders needing one board in 4Q, downgrade if elite bigs/long forwards are dominating defensive rebounding share and game is close.
If 3Q combined scoring exceeds 60 with one team shooting 50%+ and late-game foul path is live, force cashout/no-add on full-game unders.
Downgrade McDaniels/Randle rim and short-mid efficiency when Wembanyama is active and generating 4+ stocks or strong paint deterrence.

forward_model_notes: Game 4 forecast must treat Wembanyama as the series-control variable, not merely a high-usage scorer.
Minnesota can generate rebound volume and second-chance points, but efficiency suppression around the rim is still persistent.
Edwards minutes/usage are stable enough to carry MIN offense, but supporting-shot quality from Randle/McDaniels remains a risk flag.
Do not chase under positions without a larger buffer; this series can look slow early and still break totals through 3Q/4Q efficiency and free throws.
Spread bands around SAS -5.5 remain fragile because Wembanyama closeout possessions can turn a 2-4 point game into a 6-8 point final margin.
